commands.fieldguide.grant.everything.suc: Granted all Field Guide entries to %s players
commands.fieldguide.reload.success: Field Guide reloaded and synced to online players.
commands.fieldguide.revoke.everything.su: Revoked all Field Guide entries from targets
fieldguide.atmospheric.aspen_sapling.des: Home to the aspen parkland, these golden towering trees seems to watch from all sides. Don't worry though, they're harmless since the watchful logs are no different from regular logs. I think...
A thin and low hanging tree, found in the kousa jungle. Currants grow off the leaves and can be picked, but the only way to plant this tree is to have currants fall from the leaves onto the ground.
fieldguide.atmospheric.dry_laurel_saplin: Found in laurel forests or arid biomes, these short branching trees are rarely found with oranges. The saplings can be planted on sand, alongside grass, and grown as a thicker variant with four saplings.
fieldguide.atmospheric.green_aspen_sapli: Home to the aspen parkland, these lime green towering trees seems to watch from all sides. Don't worry though, they're harmless since the watchful logs are no different from regular logs. I think...
A rare tree only found as a treasure sapling in the dunes. They only grow as a four sapling setup and feature a tall twisty trunk.
fieldguide.atmospheric.kousa_sapling.des: Home to the cold kousa jungle, these trees feature blue tone leaves and wood. Kousa tress are a decent size and branch out towards the top.
fieldguide.atmospheric.laurel_sapling.de: Found in laurel forests, these short branching trees are rarely found with oranges. The saplings can be planted on sand, alongside grass, and grown as a thicker variant with four saplings.
fieldguide.atmospheric.morado_sapling.de: A short, flowering tree primarily found in the rainforest. If grown by saplings, then the tree will be able to expand further outward. Bees may use the flowering leaves to pollinate.
Rosewood's large size is due to the way it segments into other large branches, resulting in multiple tree top layers. This tree type is capable of enabling passion vines to grow passion fruit.
fieldguide.atmospheric.yucca_sapling.des: A tree that thrives in dry biomes, these feature branching tops with an occasional small hanging branch bearing fruit, as well as unique flowers on the leaves. Yucca saplings can be planted on sand alongside grass.
fieldguide.autumnity.maple_sapling.descr: A green variant of the maple tree found in forests or maple biomes. Sap can be uncovered from stripping logs, which can be collected using bottles. When near flowers, they may grow with a bee nest.
fieldguide.autumnity.orange_maple_saplin: An orange variant of the maple tree found in forests or maple biomes. Sap can be uncovered from stripping logs, which can be collected using bottles. When near flowers, they may grow with a bee nest.
fieldguide.autumnity.red_maple_sapling.d: A red variant of the maple tree found in forests or maple biomes. Sap can be uncovered from stripping logs, which can be collected using bottles. When near flowers, they may grow with a bee nest.
fieldguide.autumnity.yellow_maple_saplin: A yellow variant of the maple tree found in forests or maple biomes. Sap can be uncovered from stripping logs, which can be collected using bottles. When near flowers, they may grow with a bee nest.
fieldguide.block.minecraft\/chorus_plant: A plant-like feature that generates in the highlands of the outer End islands. They are formed in tall, coral-like patterns.
fieldguide.description.locked: Observe with a spyglass to discover details.
fieldguide.description.locked.kill: Defeat this creature to discover details.
fieldguide.description.locked.no_spyglas: Observe this closely to discover details.
fieldguide.environmental.blue_wisteria_s: Found in the rare flower forest, blue wisterias are normally woody vines that grow up and branch out into three branch segments. Those segments hold colorful droopy leaves.
fieldguide.environmental.cheerful_plum_s: Cheerful plum trees are found in blossom biomes, and have very similar variants with two other plum trees. They are large and branch out diagonally with bulky canopies.
fieldguide.environmental.moody_plum_sapl: Moody plum trees are found in blossom biomes, and have very similar variants with two other plum trees. They are large and branch out diagonally with bulky canopies.
fieldguide.environmental.pine_sapling.de: These trees are found in colder biomes. They are very tall, thin, and sometimes have knots. May rarely grow with a pinecone block.
fieldguide.environmental.pink_wisteria_s: Found in the rare flower forest, pink wisterias are normally woody vines that grow up and branch out into three branch segments. Those segments hold colorful droopy leaves.
fieldguide.environmental.plum_sapling.de: Plum trees are found in blossom biomes, and have very similar variants with two other plum trees. They are large and branch out diagonally with bulky canopies.
fieldguide.environmental.purple_wisteria: Found in the rare flower forest, purple wisterias are normally woody vines that grow up and branch out into three branch segments. Those segments hold colorful droopy leaves.
fieldguide.environmental.white_wisteria_: Found in the rare flower forest, white wisterias are normally woody vines that grow up and branch out into three branch segments. Those segments hold colorful droopy leaves.
In swamp biomes, willow trees are found. Vines hang off the leaves and are sometimes naturally seen growing in shallow water. Can grow into a larger variant.
fieldguide.fieldguide.huge_brown_mushroo: An overgrown, tall, and flat topped brown mushroom. These giants are native to the dark forest and mushroom fields. They can only be grown using bonemeal on a brown mushroom.
fieldguide.fieldguide.huge_crimson_fungu: An overgrown crimson fungus located in the crimson forest. They vary in size from small to massive, sometimes growing shroomlights under their wart cap. Can only be grown on matching nylium with bonemeal.
An overgrown, tall, and bell capped red mushroom. These giants are native to the dark forest and mushroom fields. They can only be grown using bonemeal on a red mushroom.
fieldguide.fieldguide.huge_warped_fungus: An overgrown warped fungus located in the warped forest. They vary in size from small to massive, sometimes growing shroomlights under their wart cap. Can only be grown on matching nylium with bonemeal.
fieldguide.hint.default: Observe with a spyglass to discover details.
fieldguide.hint.eat: Eat this item to discover details.
fieldguide.hint.kill: Defeat this creature to discover details.
fieldguide.hint.no_spyglass: Observe this closely to discover details.
fieldguide.minecraft.acacia_sapling.desc: Acacias are found in the savanna biome. They are somewhat tall and feature diagonal trunks, and may occasionally have multiple canopies.
fieldguide.minecraft.azalea_leaves.descr: The tree usually appears at the surface with roots consisting of rooted dirt and hanging roots that reach down to a lush cave. They can be grown from regular or flowering azalea bushes.
fieldguide.minecraft.birch_sapling.descr: Similar to oak structure, This tree is most commonly found in birch forests. They can only grow short, but can be found taller in old growth birch forests. When near flowers, they may grow with a bee nest.
fieldguide.minecraft.cherry_sapling.desc: These trees are found in cherry groves. They have distinct pink leaves and horizontally facing branches. They may occasionally have multiple canopies. When near flowers, they may grow with a bee nest.
fieldguide.minecraft.dark_oak_sapling.de: Found in the dark forest, these trees grow thicker trunks and branches that create a canopy. This filters enough light that a dense forest of them is sufficiently dark for hostile mobs to spawn during the daytime.
fieldguide.minecraft.jungle_sapling.desc: A type of tree found in rare jungle biomes. Can grow into a mega variant. Jungle trees are able to grow cocoa beans on their logs.
Mangroves are found in muddy mangrove swamps, growing in water or on land. They are grown from propagules, which can be found hanging from the trees.
fieldguide.minecraft.oak_sapling.descrip: A common tree, found in a variety of biomes. Can grow into a much larger fancier variant. When near flowers, they may grow with a bee nest.
fieldguide.minecraft.pale_oak_sapling.de: Found in the pale garden, these trees grow thicker trunks and branches that create a canopy. Pale hanging moss grows from the leaves. Sometimes these trees may contain a creaking heart within.
fieldguide.minecraft.spruce_sapling.desc: Spruce are mainly found in the taiga biome, but also appear in other colder biomes. They can also be grown into a mega variant. Spruce logs have the same texture as oak logs, but it is a darker shade of brown.
item.fieldguide.field_guide.tooltip: Right-click to open your Field Guide
item.fieldguide.page.author: Author: %s
item.fieldguide.page.tooltip: Right-click to add this entry to your Field Guide
option.fieldguide.category_scan_commands: Commands to execute when a specific category is fully scanned. Format: category_id=/command1;/command2
option.fieldguide.default_screen.tooltip: The screen shown when first opening the guide. Options: 'last_opened_screen', 'last_opened_category', 'current_biome', or a category ID (e.g., 'fieldguide:monsters').
option.fieldguide.details_silhouette_alp: The opacity of the undiscovered entry silhouettes within the details pages (0.0 - 1.0).
option.fieldguide.details_silhouette_col: The hex color code for undiscovered entry silhouettes on details pages.
option.fieldguide.details_unlocked_silho: The opacity of the discovered entry silhouettes within the details pages (0.0 - 1.0).
The hex color code for discovered entry silhouettes within the details pages.
Hides the spawn biomes grid in Field Guide entries.
option.fieldguide.disable_editing_descri: Prevents players from modifying the description of discovered entries.
Prevents players from modifying the name of discovered entries.
option.fieldguide.disable_loot_display.t: Hides the drops grid in Field Guide entries.
option.fieldguide.disable_scanning.toolt: If enabled, you will no longer be able to scan entries with spyglasses.
option.fieldguide.enable_copying_pages.t: If enabled, players can copy pages from their Field Guide to share with others.
option.fieldguide.enable_field_guide_ite: If enabled, a craftable Field Guide item will be available in the game.
option.fieldguide.enable_naked_eye_scann: Allows players to scan entries just by looking at them without a Spyglass.
option.fieldguide.enable_reliable_remove: Whether or not blacklisted items should be removed from Field Guide entries and loot displays.
option.fieldguide.enable_spyglass_scanni: Allows players to scan entries from afar using a Spyglass.
option.fieldguide.entry_scan_commands.to: Commands to execute when a specific entry is scanned. Format: entry_id=/command1;/command2
option.fieldguide.exposure.add_photograp: If enabled, a button will appear in the entry screen allowing you to attach an existing photograph from your inventory.
option.fieldguide.exposure.show_photogra: If enabled, the category grid will display a photograph instead of the standard model.
option.fieldguide.exposure.unlock_via_ph: If enabled, taking or developing a photo of entries will automatically unlock its entry in the Field Guide.
option.fieldguide.global_scan_commands.t: A list of commands to run whenever any entry is successfully scanned. Use @p for the player name.
option.fieldguide.grant_xp_on_scan.toolt: If enabled, discovering a new entry via scanning will grant the player experience points.
option.fieldguide.hide_tabs_until_unlock: If true, category tabs will not appear in the sidebar until you have unlocked at least one entry within that category.
option.fieldguide.hide_undiscovered_from: If enabled, undiscovered entries will be hidden from search results.
option.fieldguide.inventory_button_x.too: Horizontal position offset for the inventory button.
option.fieldguide.inventory_button_y.too: Vertical position offset for the inventory button.
option.fieldguide.keep_silhouette.toolti: If enabled, entries will still be displayed as silhouettes even after they've been discovered.
The opacity of the undiscovered entry silhouettes within the list pages (0.0 - 1.0).
The hex color code for undiscovered entry silhouettes within the list pages.
option.fieldguide.list_unlocked_silhouet: The opacity of the discovered entry silhouettes within the list pages (0.0 - 1.0).
The hex color code for discovered entry silhouettes within the list pages.
option.fieldguide.naked_eye_scan_distanc: The maximum distance from which you can scan an entry with your naked eye (without a Spyglass).
option.fieldguide.page_number_color.tool: The hex color code used for the page numbers.
option.fieldguide.pause_button_x.tooltip: Horizontal position offset for the pause menu button.
option.fieldguide.pause_button_y.tooltip: Vertical position offset for the pause menu button.
option.fieldguide.play_scanning_sound.to: If false, the scribbling noise played when scanning entries will be disabled.
option.fieldguide.play_unlock_sound.tool: If false, the ding sound when unlocking a new entry will be disabled.
option.fieldguide.require_item_to_open.t: If enabled, players must have the physical item to open their Field Guide.
option.fieldguide.scan_icon_x_offset.too: Adjusts the horizontal position of the scanning crosshair. Positive moves it right, negative moves it left.
option.fieldguide.scan_icon_y_offset.too: Adjusts the vertical position of the scanning crosshair. Positive moves it up, negative moves it down.
option.fieldguide.scan_overlay_alpha.too: The opacity of the scanning overlay (0.0 - 1.0).
option.fieldguide.scan_overlay_color.too: The hex color code for the scanning overlay (e.g. #FFFFFF).
option.fieldguide.scan_speed.tooltip: The time in seconds it takes to scan an entry.
If enabled, a button to open the Field Guide will appear in the player inventory.
option.fieldguide.show_out_of_range_over: Displays a red pulsing outline when aiming at targets that are too far away to scan.
option.fieldguide.show_pause_button.tool: If enabled, a button to open the Field Guide will appear in the pause menu.
option.fieldguide.show_scan_icon.tooltip: Toggles the visibility of the scanning icon near the crosshair.
option.fieldguide.show_season_icons.tool: If enabled, icons will appear on crop entries indicating which seasons they grow in.
option.fieldguide.show_toasts.tooltip: Whether to show a toast notification when a new entry is discovered.
option.fieldguide.show_undiscovered_name: If enabled, names of undiscovered entries will be visible.
option.fieldguide.show_unlock_date.toolt: If enabled, a small quill icon will appear in the top right of discovered entries. Hovering over it will display the date and time the entry was unlocked.
option.fieldguide.spyglass_scan_distance: The maximum distance from which you can scan an entry while using a Spyglass.
option.fieldguide.text_color.tooltip: The hex color code used for standard text in the field guide.
option.fieldguide.text_cursor_color.tool: Hex color code for the text cursor.
option.fieldguide.text_muted_color.toolt: The hex color code used for muted/secondary text.
option.fieldguide.text_title_color.toolt: The hex color code used for titles in the field guide.
option.fieldguide.unlock_variants.toolti: If enabled, all variants of an entry will be unlocked after they've been discovered.
option.fieldguide.use_real_world_date.to: If true, discovery dates are shown using the real-world date instead of the in-game day.
option.fieldguide.xp_amount_on_scan.tool: The amount of experience points granted when an entry is successfully scanned for the first time.
